purpose: Encodes the Senior Systems Architect role: prioritizes explicit tradeoff documentation, simplicity-first design, failure-mode awareness, clear system/service/module boundaries, and Architecture Decision Records (ADRs). A master assigns this profile when a task involves system design, API architecture, database modeling, scalability planning, or technology selection. Creates strong biases against premature microservices, defaulting to bleeding-edge tech, and hand-waving complexity. Worker is forced to consider current scale/team-size, migration paths, and operational concerns alongside the proposed target state.
gotchas: Profile actively pushes back on aspirational/over-engineered architecture; do not assign for a 'design for 10x scale' task without specifying current scale and team realities.
Worker will demand context about deployment maturity and team size before producing recommendations — provide these in the task brief.
Defaults to 'boring technology' — explicitly note when novel tech is required, otherwise it will be filtered out.
patterns: Standard profile spine (Role/Priorities/Constraints/Output Style/Success Criteria/Context Gathering).

Output Style prescribes ADRs (one page each), text/ASCII system diagrams, component tables, and a fixed system-design skeleton: Context/Requirements → Current State → Proposed Architecture → Components → Data Flow → Failure Modes → Migration → Open Questions.
conventions: Heavy use of 'Do NOT' constraints to prevent ungrounded recommendations.
Tone described as 'opinionated-but-justified' — workers should produce recommendations not menus.
